changed.summary: Added watch command for continuous monitoring of source files with configurable debouncing and session management
changed.before: Provided review, serve, analyze, and init commands for static topology operations
structural_changes: Added import: startWatchServer from watch-server module
Added command: watch with topology, delta, base, port, debounce, and fresh options

changed.summary: Introduced timeline data structures to track per-node change statistics across time
changed.before: Defined Topology and SystemDelta schemas for single-point analysis
structural_changes: Added type: TimelineNodeStat (tracks filesChanged, linesAdded, linesRemoved, files per node)
Added type: TimelineEntry (timestamp, base ref, nodes array)

intent: Add live file watching mode that tracks structural changes over time in a timeline view

decision_trace.decision: Add watch mode as separate command rather than extending serve
alternatives: Add --watch flag to existing serve command
Create unified dev mode combining serve and watch
decision_trace.rationale: Watch mode focuses on timeline tracking of structural changes while serve mode focuses on interactive editing. Separation maintains clear boundaries and allows independent evolution.

decision_trace.decision: Model timeline as session with entries rather than flat log
alternatives: Single flat array of change events
decision_trace.rationale: Session-based timeline preserves context across restarts and enables pattern analysis over time. The fresh flag allows starting new sessions while retaining historical data.

decision_trace.decision: Make debounce interval configurable via command option
alternatives: Immediate analysis on every file change
decision_trace.rationale: Configurable debouncing (default 1500ms) batches rapid successive edits into single timeline entries, reducing noise and analysis overhead while staying responsive.

decision_trace.decision: Track statistics per topology node rather than per file
decision_trace.rationale: Node-level aggregation aligns with architectural viewpoint and identifies which system components are actively evolving, abstracting away file-level implementation details.
